verses.text: The LORD: make two silver trumpets of one piece — for assembly and journeying. Both — congregation gathers. One — princes only. Alarm — east journeys; second — south. Aaron's sons blow them. In war, blow alarm — be remembered, saved. In gladness, feasts, new moons — blow over offerings; a memorial before God. Guidance: Two trumpets do everything. A long blast gathers; broken alarm moves. The same notes that summon worship summon war. God's people learn to hear what the air is telling them.

On the twentieth day of the second month, second year, the cloud was taken up from off the tabernacle. Israel took their journeys out of the wilderness of Sinai; the cloud rested in the wilderness of Paran. First journey at the commandment of the LORD by Moses. Guidance: Almost a year at Sinai — now the cloud lifts. God taught them how to camp, how to worship; now the walk begins. Holy waiting prepares the body for holy walking.

Order of departure: first Judah's standard — Nahshon. Tabernacle taken down; Gershon and Merari set forward bearing it. Second Reuben's standard — Elizur. Kohath set forward bearing the sanctuary. Third Ephraim's standard — Elishama. Fourth Dan's standard, rearguard — Ahiezer. Guidance: Judah leads, the camp follows in tribal order, the holy things travel in the middle protected on both sides. Even the lion tribe walks behind the cloud. Worship sets the pace of war.

Moses to Hobab his father-in-law: come with us to the place the LORD will give us. Hobab: I will go to my own land. Moses: leave us not, thou knowest how to encamp here, and shalt be eyes to us. What good the LORD does us, we will do to thee. Guidance: Moses pleads with his Midianite father-in-law to come. God's promise to Israel doesn't shut out the foreigner. Be our eyes, says Moses to one outsider. Mission begins in a tent.

Departed from the mount three days' journey; the ark went before them to search out a resting place. The cloud over them by day. When the ark set forward Moses said: Rise up, LORD, let Thine enemies be scattered. When it rested: Return, O LORD, to the many thousands of Israel. Guidance: Two prayers braid through every march: rise and return. The ark goes ahead to find rest; the cloud covers the camp. God leads from the front and gathers from behind.
